title: Pentecost Sunday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: June 5, 2022

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon was preached on Whit Sunday (Pentecost), the 50th day after Easter, celebrating the descent of the Holy Spirit upon the apostles. The lectionary readings for this date under the Revised Common Lectionary would have been Acts 2:1-21 (the Pentecost account), Psalm 104:24-34,35b, 1 Corinthians 12:3b-13 (the gifts of the Spirit), and John 7:37-39 (rivers of living water). This falls within the Season of Easter, specifically its closing commemoration before the transition into Ordinary Time.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the immediate aftermath of its 80th General Convention, held in July 2022, meaning leadership and dioceses were actively preparing for this major gathering scheduled for Baltimore in six weeks. The Diocese of Colorado had been navigating post-pandemic recovery and attendance rebuilding across its parishes. No major ecclesiastical controversies or bishop elections were active in the Rocky Mountain dioceses during this specific week.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was in the early summer season with typical warm weather patterns. The Colorado primary election had taken place on June 28, 2022, but this sermon predated that event by three weeks. The city was actively reopening venues and normalizing public life after two years of COVID-19 pandemic restrictions. No major weather events or crises were reported in the Denver metropolitan area the week of June 5, 2022.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The week of June 5, 2022 was dominated by the January 6 Committee's first televised public hearing, held on June 9, detailing findings about the Capitol riot with testimony from Cassidy Hutchinson and others. The US Supreme Court had recently overturned Roe v. Wade on June 24, 2022, but that decision came after this sermon date. Inflation remained a dominant economic concern with the Federal Reserve preparing for interest rate hikes. The Buffalo supermarket shooting on May 14 had occurred less than three weeks prior, keeping gun violence in national discourse.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's invasion of Ukraine, which began on February 24, 2022, was now over three months underway with no resolution in sight and significant Western sanctions in place. NATO was actively expanding its presence in Eastern Europe with Finland and Sweden pursuing membership applications in response to Russian aggression. Humanitarian crises in Ukraine were generating global media coverage and relief efforts.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture was experiencing significant political polarization with the January 6 Committee hearings intensifying partisan divisions. The nation was simultaneously processing grief and anger following the Buffalo shooting while awaiting major Supreme Court decisions on abortion and other constitutional matters. Sports culture was in an interseason moment (NBA Finals ongoing, MLB in full swing), with Denver's sports teams in their respective seasons. There was a general sense of national turbulence and uncertainty about the direction of American institutions and democracy.
